data.title: Greeks.live: BTC price volatility keeps most traders cautious, community sentiment seems to be generally bearish
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Greeks.live macro researcher Adam released an English community briefing, pointing out that after experiencing significant losses, community sentiment seems to be generally bearish, and traders are closely watching the $82,000 price level as a key support. There are some disagreements in the community, and a few members believe that there may be an increase in the next few months, but the recent sharp price fluctuations from $95,000 to $78,000 have made most traders cautious. After the sudden sell-off, most traders are in recovery mode, and many have to realize losses rather than roll over positions due to margin restrictions.

data.title: XRP Price Makes U-Turn With 13% Surge, What Comes Next?
data.description:
XRP skyrockets 13% as March kicks off on positive tone

data.title: DWF transferred 2.5 million AXL to the suspected Axelar team address, equivalent to about 1.11 million US dollars
data.description: Arkham monitoring shows that the DWF Labs address has transferred 2.5 million AXL, equivalent to about 1.11 million US dollars, to the address marked as "suspected Axelar Network" by Arkham two days ago. Based on the transfer records, it is speculated that the two parties may have ended the market-making cooperation plan. The actual situation needs to wait for the DWF Labs and Axelar Network teams to update.

data.title: A new address withdrew 1,000 BTC from Binance
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows that a new address withdrew 1,000 BTC from Binance, about 84.4 million US dollars.

data.title: Traders Cautious Amid Market Volatility
data.description: According to PANews, Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks.live, released a briefing in the English community highlighting a prevailing bearish sentiment following significant losses. Traders are closely monitoring the $82,000 level as a critical support point. While there is some division within the community, with a few members anticipating a potential rise in the coming months, the recent sharp price fluctuation from $95,000 to $78,000 has led most traders to adopt a cautious approach. After the sudden sell-off, many traders are in recovery mode, with margin constraints forcing them to realize losses rather than extend positions.

data.title: Metis launches Hyperion, a new high-performance chain dedicated to AI, and will launch a test network before April this year
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Natalia of the Metis Foundation announced at ETH Denver that Metis has launched a new high-performance Layer 2 chain, Metis Hyperion. While the new chain is fully compatible with Ethereum, it provides zero-compromise security, seamless scalability, and enterprise-level system reliability for AI applications.
The Metis Hyperion testnet will be launched between February and April 2025, including high-performance sorting nodes, initial developer tools, and framework testing capabilities. The mainnet will be delivered between May and August 2025, providing a high-throughput Layer 2 network and a complete developer kit in a production environment.

data.title: ZachXBT: An unknown victim was attacked by the North Korean hacker Lazarus Group on Tron and lost about $3.1 million
data.description: "Chain Detective" ZachXBT said in his personal Telegram channel that an unknown victim was attacked by North Korean hacker Lazarus Group on Tron on February 28 and lost about 3.1 million US dollars. The funds have been transferred from Tron to Ethereum, and ETH was divided into ten addresses before being deposited in Tornado Cash.

data.title: North Korean Hackers Allegedly Steal $3.1 Million In Crypto Heist
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain investigator ZachXBT reported on his personal Telegram channel that an unidentified victim suffered a cyberattack by the North Korean hacker group Lazarus on February 28. The attack occurred on the Tron network, resulting in a loss of approximately $3.1 million. The stolen funds were subsequently moved from Tron to Ethereum, where they were distributed across ten addresses before being deposited into Tornado Cash.
